metadata.name: pyunifi
metadata.summary: API for Ubiquity Networks UniFi controller

modules.name: pyunifi
documentation: Python __init__ to interact with UniFi Controller

functions.name: pyunifi.http_debug_log_stderr
documentation: Dump requests urllib3 debug messages to stderr

modules.name: pyunifi.controller
documentation: Python package to interact with UniFi Controller

functions.name: pyunifi.controller.retry_login
documentation: To reattempt login if requests exception(s) occur at time of call

classes.name: pyunifi.controller.APIError
documentation: API Error exceptions

classes.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.__init__
documentation:
---
param host: the address of the controller host; IP or name
param password: the password to log in with
param port: the port of the controller host
param site_id: the site ID to connect to
param ssl_verify: |-
  Verify the controllers SSL certificate,
  can also be "path/to/custom_cert.pem"
param username: the username to log in with
param version: the base version of the controller API [v4|v5]
---

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.switch_site
documentation:
Switch to another site

```
param name

Site Name

return

True or APIError
```

documentation: Return a list of Alerts unarchived.

Return a list of all APs,

Return a list of all active clients,

Return a list of all sites,

Return a list of configured WLANs

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.get_device_stat
documentation:
Gets the current state & configuration of
the given device based on its MAC Address.
\:param target_mac: MAC address of the device.
\:type target_mac: str
\:returns: Dictionary containing metadata, state,

> capabilities and configuration of the device

```
rtype

dict()
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.get_radius_users
documentation:
Return a list of all users, with their
name, password, 24 digit user id, and 24 digit site id

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.add_radius_user
documentation:
Add a new user with this username and password
\:param name: new user's username
\:param password: new user's password
\:returns: user's name, password, 24 digit user id, and 24 digit site id

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.update_radius_user
documentation:
Update a user to this new username and password
\:param name: user's new username
\:param password: user's new password
\:param id: the user's 24 digit user id, from get_radius_users()

> or add_radius_user()

```
returns

user's name, password, 24 digit user id, and 24 digit site id

returns

[] if no change was made
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.delete_radius_user
documentation:
Delete user
\:param id: the user's 24 digit user id, from get_radius_users()

> or add_radius_user()

```
returns

[] if successful
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.get_switch_port_overrides
documentation:
Gets a list of port overrides, in dictionary
format, for the given target MAC address. The
dictionary contains the port_idx, portconf_id,
poe_mode, & name.

```
param target_mac

MAC address of the device.

type target_mac

str

returns

[ { 'port_idx': int(), 'portconf': str,
'poe_mode': str, 'name': str } ]

rtype

list( dict() )
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller._switch_port_power
documentation:
Helper method to set the given PoE mode the port/switch.

```
param target_mac

MAC address of the Switch.

type target_mac

str

param port_idx

Port ID to target

type port_idx

int

param mode

PoE mode to set. ie. auto, on, off.

type mode

str

returns

{ 'port_overrides': [ { 'port_idx': int(),
'portconf': str, 'poe_mode': str, 'name': str } ] }

rtype

dict( list( dict() ) )
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.switch_port_power_off
documentation:
Powers Off the given port on the Switch identified
by the given MAC Address.

```
param target_mac

MAC address of the Switch.

type target_mac

str

param port_idx

Port ID to power off

type port_idx

int

returns

API Response which is the resulting complete port overrides

rtype

list( dict() )
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.switch_port_power_on
documentation:
Powers On the given port on the Switch identified
by the given MAC Address.

```
param target_mac

MAC address of the Switch.

type target_mac

str

param port_idx

Port ID to power on

type port_idx

int

returns

API Response which is the resulting complete port overrides

rtype

list( dict() )
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.create_site
documentation:
Create a new site.

```
param desc

Name of the site to be created.
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.block_client
documentation:
Add a client to the block list.

```
param mac

the MAC address of the client to block.
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.unblock_client
documentation:
Remove a client from the block list.

```
param mac

the MAC address of the client to unblock.
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.disconnect_client
documentation:
Disconnect a client.

Disconnects a client, forcing them to reassociate. Useful when the
connection is of bad quality to force a rescan.

```
param mac

the MAC address of the client to disconnect.
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.restart_ap
documentation:
Restart an access point (by MAC).

```
param mac

the MAC address of the AP to restart.
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.restart_ap_name
documentation:
Restart an access point (by name).

```
param name

the name address of the AP to restart.
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.archive_all_alerts
documentation: Archive all Alerts

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.create_backup
documentation:
Ask controller to create a backup archive file

..warning:

: This process puts significant load on the controller
  and may render it partially unresponsive for other requests.

```
param days

metrics of the last x days will be added to the backup.
'-1' backup all metrics. '0' backup only the configuration.

return

URL path to backup file
```
TODO: Not currently supported on UDMP as it now utilizes async-backups.

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.get_backup
documentation:
---
param download_path: |-
  path to backup; if None is given
  one will be created
param target_file: |-
  Filename or full path to download the
  backup archive to, should have .unf extension for restore.
---
TODO: Not currently supported on UDMP as it now utilizes async-backups.

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.authorize_guest
documentation:
Authorize a guest based on his MAC address.

```
param guest_mac

the guest MAC address: 'aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff'

param minutes

duration of the authorization in minutes

param up_bandwidth

up speed allowed in kbps

param down_bandwidth

down speed allowed in kbps

param byte_quota

quantity of bytes allowed in MB

param ap_mac

access point MAC address
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.unauthorize_guest
documentation:
Unauthorize a guest based on his MAC address.

```
param guest_mac

the guest MAC address: 'aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff'
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.get_firmware
documentation:
Return a list of available/cached firmware versions

```
param cached

Return cached firmwares

param available

Return available (and not cached) firmwares

param known

Return only firmwares for known devices

param site

Return only firmwares for on-site devices

return

List of firmware dicts
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.cache_firmware
documentation:
Cache the firmware on the UniFi Controller

:::{warning}
Caching one device might very well cache others,
as they're on shared platforms
:::

```
param version

version to cache

param device

device model to cache (e.g. BZ2)

return

True/False
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.remove_firmware
documentation:
Remove cached firmware from the UniFi Controller

:::{warning}
Removing one device's firmware might very well remove
others, as they're on shared platforms
:::

```
param version

version to cache

param device

device model to cache (e.g. BZ2)

return

True/false
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.get_tag
documentation: Get all tags and their member MACs

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.upgrade_device
documentation:
Upgrade a device's firmware to verion

\:param version: version to upgrade to

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.provision
documentation:
Force provisioning of a device

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.get_setting
documentation:
Return settings for this site or controller

Return only controller-wide settings

Only return this/these section(s)

return

{section:settings}
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.update_setting
documentation:
Update settings

```

{section:{settings}}

return

resulting settings
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.update_user_group
documentation:
Update user group bandwidth settings

Group ID to modify

param down_kbps

New bandwidth in KBPS for download

New bandwidth in KBPS for upload
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.set_client_alias
documentation:
Set the client alias. Set to "" to reset to default
\:param mac: The MAC of the client to rename
\:param alias: The alias to set

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.create_voucher
documentation:
Create voucher for guests.

```
param number

number of vouchers

param quota

number of using; 0 = unlimited

param expire

expiration of voucher in minutes

param up_bandwidth

up speed allowed in kbps

param down_bandwidth

down speed allowed in kbps

param byte_quota

quantity of bytes allowed in MB

param note

description
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.list_vouchers
documentation:
Get list of vouchers

```
param filter_voucher

Filter vouchers by create_time, code, quota,
used, note, status_expires, status, ...
```

methods.name: pyunifi.controller.Controller.delete_voucher
documentation:
Delete / revoke voucher

```
param id

id of voucher
```

Interact with a UniFi controller.

Uses the JSON interface on port 8443 (HTTPS) to communicate with a UniFi
controller. Operations will raise unifi.controller.APIError on obvious
problems (such as login failure), but many errors (such as disconnecting a
nonexistant client) will go unreported.

```pycon
>>> from unifi.controller import Controller
>>> c = Controller('192.168.1.99', 'admin', 'p4ssw0rd')
>>> for ap in c.get_aps():
...     print 'AP named %s with MAC %s' % (ap.get('name'), ap['mac'])
...
AP named Study with MAC dc:9f:db:1a:59:07
AP named Living Room with MAC dc:9f:db:1a:59:08
AP named Garage with MAC dc:9f:db:1a:59:0b
```
